assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 7-13: Version Impact Table shows 2.1.0 (h2 0.4.5) as 'YES', 2.1.1 (h2 0.4.5) as 'YES', and 2.2.0 through 2.2.4 (h2 0.4.8 or 0.4.9) all as 'NO'. Each entry cites the h2 version found in the lock file at the pinned source commit.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md lines 27-29: 'Proposed: [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1]' — only 2.1.x versions included. Line 33: 'Remove: RHTPA 2.2.0 -- lock file analysis confirms h2 0.4.8 (fixed version, not vulnerable)'. The proposed Jira mutation (lines 40-45) lists only RHTPA 2.1.0 and RHTPA 2.1.1.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 7 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 5: 'Case A: Affected -- create remediation tasks for the 2.1.x stream only.' Task 1 (lines 19-78) targets upstream branch release/0.3.z (2.1.x stream). Task 2 (lines 84-137) targets rhtpa-release.0.3.z (2.1.x downstream). Lines 164-176: 'No Remediation for 2.2.x Stream' with table confirming all 2.2.x versions ship h2 >= 0.4.8 and 'No tasks are created for this stream.'

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 9: 'Product version (PSIRT-claimed) | (no stream suffix -- unscoped)'. Lines 25-26: 'This issue is unscoped -- it covers all configured version streams.' Line 29: 'Both streams will be analyzed in Step 2.' version-impact.md confirms all versions checked: 2.1.0, 2.1.1 (2.1.x stream) and 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4 (2.2.x stream).

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 7 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 11-12: 'Since the issue is unscoped (no stream suffix), there is no cross-stream impact notice to generate. The unscoped issue already covers all streams, so Case B (cross-stream impact) does not apply.' No cross-stream impact notice or sibling issue creation appears anywhere in the output files.
